seo.title: Building SPA indexing and rendering strategies with React...
seo.description: A step-by-step implementation guide for SPA indexing and rendering strategies with React and Vue.

content.intro: This guide provides actionable steps to implement SEO for single-page applications (SPAs) using React, Vue, or Angular. Focuses on resolving indexing issues, dynamic content rendering, and crawlability challenges specific to client-rendered frameworks.
prerequisites: SPA built with React, Vue, or Angular
Basic understanding of HTTP status codes

steps.title: Configure server-side rendering (SSR) or prerendering
steps.explanation: Implement SSR using Next.js/Nuxt or prerendering with Prerender.io. Ensure server returns 200 status code for all routes and serves pre-rendered HTML. Use framework-specific SSR middleware for React/Angular.
pitfalls: Forgetting to set proper HTTP headers for prerendered content
Not handling 404 routes in SSR setup

steps.title: Implement dynamic meta tags and Open Graph data
steps.explanation: Use framework-specific meta tag managers (React Helmet, vue-meta) to set page-specific title, description, and Open Graph tags. Ensure values update on route changes and match content.
pitfalls: Not updating meta tags during client-side navigation
Using static meta values across all pages

steps.title: Validate URL structure and navigation
steps.explanation: Ensure all client routes use proper HTTP 200 responses and match server-rendered content. Implement hash-based or HTML5 history navigation consistently across the app.
pitfalls: Using hash-based URLs without 301 redirects to canonical versions
Not testing deep linking in SPA routing

steps.title: Test crawlability with Googlebot
steps.explanation: Use Google Search Console's URL Inspection tool to verify how Googlebot renders pages. Check for JavaScript errors in the console and ensure all content is visible in the rendered HTML.

code.content:
curl -I https://your-spa-domain.com/your-page
# Verify response code 200 and check for rendered HTML content

pitfalls: Ignoring JavaScript execution errors in crawl tests
Not verifying content visibility in Google's rendered view

steps.title: Set up server-side redirects for legacy URLs
steps.explanation: Configure server to handle 301 redirects for old URLs when migrating from traditional server-rendered apps. Use middleware to maintain SEO value during SPA transitions.
pitfalls: Forgetting to update sitemap.xml after redirects

content.conclusion: After implementing these steps, verify crawlability with Google Search Console and monitor organic traffic patterns. Regularly audit dynamic content rendering and ensure SSR/prerendering pipelines remain aligned with application changes.
